hero_subtitle: A historic and spiritual sacred place in Jamalpur
about_text: Doyamoyee Temple, located at Zero Point in Jamalpur, is a historic Hindu temple established in 1698 (1104 Bengali Era) during the reign of Nawab Murshid Quli Khan. Founded by Jagirdar Sri Krishna Roy Chowdhury, the temple complex includes shrines dedicated to Shiva, Kali, Nat Mandir, and Manasa Devi. It is renowned for its centuries-old murals and traditional architectural heritage.
history_text: For over 321 years, the temple has remained a center of daily worship and spiritual devotion. During the annual Ashtami Fair, devotees from across the country gather in large numbers. The Debottor Estate was established by Rani Shrimati Narayani Roy Chowdhury, and the temple’s structural development was later enhanced by Raja Jogendra Kishore Roy Chowdhury. The temple remains open daily from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM.
founder: Founder: Jagirdar Sri Krishna Roy Chowdhury
estate_founder: Debottor Estate Founder: Rani Shrimati Narayani Roy Chowdhury
development: Infrastructure Development: Raja Jogendra Kishore Roy Chowdhury
events_text: Doyamoyee Temple holds Durga Puja, Ashtami Fair, and annual Chaitra festivals. Each event will feature a countdown timer.
volunteer_text: If you wish to participate as a volunteer at Doyamoyee Temple, please fill out the form below.
blogs_text: Stay updated with the latest news, events, and important announcements from Doyamoyee Temple. Explore our articles, festivals, and spiritual insights to keep connected with the temple community.
notice_text: View recent announcements, puja schedules, and important notifications here.

footer_about_text: Sri Sri Ri Doyamoyee Temple is a sacred place of worship, devotion and spiritual peace located in Jamalpur. Devotees gather here for puja, festivals and community service.
